semanticCore: Shivering, shuddering of the skin, goosebumps from fear/awe, or hair standing on end

keyDerivations.meaning: To shiver, shudder, experience goosebumps, or tremble from fear/awe

figurativeOrRareUsages: اللَّهُ نَزَّلَ أَحْسَنَ الْحَدِيثِ كِتَابًا مُتَشَابِهًا مَثَانِيَ تَقْشَعِرُّ مِنْهُ جُلُودُ الَّذِينَ يَخْشَوْنَ رَبَّهُمْ (39:23): God has sent down the best statement... whereat shiver the skins of those who fear their Lord.
summaryEn: The root signifies physical shuddering from awe (Qash'ar), describing the profound physiological response of believers experiencing the power of Quranic recitation.
summaryAr: يمثل هذا الجذر اقشعار الجلد وارتعاده ووقوف الشعر هيبة ورهبة (قشعر)، مصوراً الأثر الحسي العظيم لقراءة القرآن على قلوب وأجساد خاشعي الله.
rawText: أَخَذَتْهُ قُشَعْرِيرَةٌ (S, K) A tremor, quaking, or quivering, of the skin seized him. (K.) إِقْشَعَرَّ , said of the skin, (S, K,) It quaked; shuddered; was, or became, affected by a tremor, quaking, or quivering. (K.) [And in like manner said of a man, (see the part. n., below,) i. e. He quaked, or shuddered.] === It (the skin) dried up (قَفَّ) by reason of mange, or scab. (TA.) === إِقْشَعَرَّتِ الأَرْضُ The earth became of a colour inclining to that of dust, or ashes, (إِرْبَدَّت,) and contracted, by reason of drought. (TA.) === اقشعرّت السَّنَةُ ‡ The year became one of drought. (K, * TA:) قشعر قُشَاعِرٌ Rough to the touch; خَشِنُ المَسِّ: (K, accord. to the TA:) or rough, and advanced in years; خَشِنٌ مُسِنٌّ, with the art. الخَشِنُ المُسِنُّ. (CK, and a MS. copy of the K.) مُقْشَعِرٌّ , applied to a man, [Having a quaking or shuddering of the skin,] has for its pl. قَشَاعِرُ, without the م because it is augmentative. (S.)
